expect: B-EVAL1 falsification criterion (r>0.8 health vs external validation over >=20 sessions) is unmeasurable because neither time series exists. This makes B-EVAL1 unfalsifiable by neglect, not by design.
actual: Confirmed. maintenance-outcomes.json has 2 sessions (S534-S535). human_impact.py has no per-session time series. Zero external validation data in 535 sessions. Falsification criterion is structurally unmeasurable.
diff: As expected — no surprise. The challenge is VALID.
proposed_fix: Add per-session benefit_ratio to maintenance-outcomes.json (requires human_impact.py integration)
Add per-session health composite score to maintenance-outcomes.json
After 20 sessions of both, compute r(health, benefit_ratio) to actually test falsification criterion
Interim: downgrade B-EVAL1 evidence to 'observed (internal half only)'
conclusion: B-EVAL1 claim is likely true but epistemically dishonest in its evidence labeling. 'Observed' implies measurement when the comparative half (external validation) has never been measured. Challenge filed in CHALLENGES.md.
